title: Iterators 4
description: The `filter()` adapter keeps only elements that match a predicate.
instructions: Use filter to select elements from a collection.
hints: filter takes a predicate closure that returns bool: .filter(|x| condition)
With filter on iter(), the closure receives &&i32, so use **x or |&&x|.
Chain filter and map: .filter(|&&x| x % 2 != 0).map(|&x| x * x)
explanation: The `filter()` method creates an iterator that only yields elements for which the predicate returns `true`. Note that filter passes references to the closure (to avoid moving elements), so with `iter()` you get `&&T`. You can destructure with `|&&x|` or dereference with `|x| **x % 2 == 0`. Chaining filter and map is a powerful functional programming pattern.
